name: farmhouse-kitchen
summary: The working kitchen of a farmhouse — oven, prep table, hanging herbs, a well-worn butcher's block. Where food is actually made, not eaten.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle, often adjacent to pantry or stillroom

primary_palette.note: bread-kitchen — the oven is the workhorse, used daily
butcher-kitchen — chopping block dominates, meat ages overhead
prep-table kitchen — the big worktable is where it all happens
soup-and-stew kitchen — the cauldron is always going

story_prompts: mid-baking — dough rising on the table, the oven just fired
after a feast — wreckage of plates and pots, one exhausted cook asleep in a chair
the slaughter day — the block is bloody, a pig hangs, the family works in shifts
the cook has been poisoned and the kitchen is being quietly searched
the kitchen has been abandoned — mice in the flour, rust on the cleaver, a calendar on the wall

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: warm working (0.4-0.5) — oven glow, hearth fire, window if any

anti_patterns: this is a WORK kitchen, not a dining room — no long-table of chairs
the surfaces are USED — chopping block stained, table floured, pots heavy with char
herbs hang from walls and rafters (described in prose), props go on horizontal surfaces
avoid formal decoration — even a noble kitchen is utilitarian downstairs
